ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a wheel of fortune website where users can spin the wheel to win prizes. The website should have functionalities for spinning the wheel, displaying prizes, and recording user winning records. Users should be able to spin the wheel, view the prize list, view their own winning records. Use light gray as the default background and dark red for component styling.

evaluation_checklist.question: Spin the wheel to initiate a gameplay session.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The wheel spins, stops at a segment, and displays the prize won without any errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: View the list of available prizes before spinning the wheel.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A complete and accurate list of prizes is displayed, matching the set prizes available on the wheel.

evaluation_checklist.question: After spinning the wheel, navigate to the section displaying user winning records.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user's winning record is updated to include the latest prize won, with correct details on date and prize.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'light gray' as the background color and 'dark red' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'light gray' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark red', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

ui_instruct.task: Spin the wheel to initiate a gameplay session.
ui_instruct.expected_result: The wheel spins, stops at a segment, and displays the prize won without any errors.

ui_instruct.task: View the list of available prizes before spinning the wheel.
ui_instruct.expected_result: A complete and accurate list of prizes is displayed, matching the set prizes available on the wheel.

ui_instruct.task: After spinning the wheel, navigate to the section displaying user winning records.
ui_instruct.expected_result: The user's winning record is updated to include the latest prize won, with correct details on date and prize.

ui_instruct.task: Verify that the website uses 'light gray' as the background color and 'dark red' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
ui_instruct.expected_result: The website has a background color of 'light gray' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark red', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

oracle_slots.expected_result: The user's winning record is updated to include the latest prize won, with correct details on date and prize.

oracle_slots.expected_result: The website has a background color of 'light gray' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark red', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

oracle_slots.expected_result: No unrequested elements or functionalities should be present on the page.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictability, wouldn’t it be fun to create a wheel of fortune website? Imagine users spinning a wheel to win prizes, like a fun little game to brighten their day! They could spin the wheel, and oh, I heard from a friend that they won a gift card last week, which got me thinking about how exciting it would be to display a list of prizes on this site. And, of course, we should definitely let users keep track of their winning records, just like how I keep track of my favorite recipes. By the way, I had this amazing dinner last night, and it made me realize how important it is to have a nice light gray background on the site, so it’s easy on the eyes, and maybe we can style the components in a dark red to make everything pop! But honestly, I can’t stop thinking about how my neighbor is always complaining about their lawn. Anyway, back to the website! Users really should be able to spin the wheel, view the prize list, and check their winning records. It’ll be a blast!

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a customer relationship management website for managing sales leads and reporting. The website should have functionalities for automated message sending, organizing and categorizing leads, updating lead information and status, updating settings and dropdown menus, and reporting. Users should be able to browse and manage sales leads, view reports, and receive best practice and integration advice. Define background as papaya whip; define components with dark orange.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the "Add New Lead" form allows users to fill out and submit details correctly.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The form submissions successfully add the lead to the system.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the automated message-sending functionality by initiating a message to a specific lead.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The message is sent automatically, and a confirmation notification is displayed.

evaluation_checklist.question: Confirm that lead information can be updated by modifying an existing lead's status.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The lead’s status is successfully updated, and the new status is accurately displayed on the lead’s profile.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test website navigation by using the provided menu to access the best practice and integration advice section.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The relevant page is successfully loaded, and users can view the information on best practices and integration advice.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'papaya whip' as the background color and 'dark orange' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'papaya whip' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark orange', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Speaking of unpredictability, have you ever tried to implement a customer relationship management website? It's like managing sales leads can feel like herding cats sometimes! Anyway, I heard that the new café downtown has the best pastries; I can't wait to check it out after work. So, back to the website, right? It should totally have these functionalities like automated message sending, which is super crucial because who has time to send messages manually? And organizing leads, oh boy, that's like organizing your sock drawer! You definitely want to categorize them too, like how I categorize my favorite TV shows. Updating lead info is a must; it’s like keeping your plants watered, you know? And settings? Don’t even get me started on dropdown menus! I mean, have you seen how many options some of these websites have? It’s like a buffet of choices! And reports, yes, those are important too, like when you need to impress your boss after a long day of dealing with competitors who just don’t get it. Oh, and speaking of colors, I really think background colors can set the mood; like, have you ever tried papaya whip? It’s such a cheerful color! And dark orange for components? That sounds vibrant! Anyway, I really hope the café has those pastries; they say a good pastry can make your day so much better!
